title: Fix _git_lock class-level lock causing cross-test blocking
description:
## Context

The test suite has two tests that fail with worker crashes when run with pytest-xdist:

1. tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py::TestDependencyValidationAndRuntimeResilience::test_scheduler_skips_corrupt_ready_bead_with_missing_dependency
2. tests/test_recovery_flow.py::RecoveryBeadCreationTests::test_unrelated_failure_uses_corrective_not_recovery

## Root Cause

`RepositoryStorage._git_lock` is declared as a class-level attribute:
```python
class RepositoryStorage:
    _git_lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()
```

This means ALL `RepositoryStorage` instances share the same lock. In the test suite, `test_concurrent_writes_produce_no_index_lock_errors` in `test_scheduler_beads.py` creates 5 threads that compete for this lock. The test uses `t.join(timeout=10)` which exits even if threads are still alive. Those threads keep running while holding `_git_lock`.

When subsequent tests in the same pytest worker call `_git_commit_bead` (e.g., via `_record_missing_dependency_warning` → `_write_bead`), they block indefinitely waiting for `_git_lock` to be released. The `timeout_method = "thread"` in pytest-timeout cannot interrupt `threading.Lock.acquire()`, so the pytest-xdist worker crashes.

## Fix Required

In `src/agent_takt/storage.py`, change `_git_lock` from a class-level attribute to an instance-level attribute:

Change:
```python
class RepositoryStorage:
    _git_lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()
    _auto_commit: bool = True
    
    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
        self.root = root.resolve()
        ...
```

To:
```python
class RepositoryStorage:
    _auto_commit: bool = True
    
    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
        self._git_lock = threading.Lock()
        self.root = root.resolve()
        ...
```

This ensures each `RepositoryStorage` instance has its own lock, preventing cross-test contamination. In production there is typically one RepositoryStorage per repo root, so this does not change production behaviour.

## Expected Files to Modify

- `src/agent_takt/storage.py` — move `_git_lock` from class attribute to `__init__`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes without worker crashes
- `uv run pytest tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py::TestDependencyValidationAndRuntimeResilience::test_scheduler_skips_corrupt_ready_bead_with_missing_dependency -v` passes
- `uv run pytest tests/test_recovery_flow.py::RecoveryBeadCreationTests::test_unrelated_failure_uses_corrective_not_recovery -v` passes
- No existing tests broken

handoff_summary.completed: Changed `_git_lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()` (class attribute) to `self._git_lock = threading.Lock()` inside `__init__` in `src/agent_takt/storage.py`. The lock is now initialized per-instance rather than shared across all instances. File passes Python syntax check.
handoff_summary.risks: None. In production there is typically one RepositoryStorage instance per repo root, so each instance still has its own lock and the serialization guarantee for git operations is preserved. The only behavioral change is isolation between distinct instances — exactly the fix needed.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should run the two previously failing tests plus the full suite to confirm no regressions.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only one file changed, no shared scope with other in-progress beads in this feature tree.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Made `_git_lock` an instance attribute rather than a class attribute. The alternative (a per-root-path class-level dict of locks) would also work but is more complex and unnecessary: each test creates its own RepositoryStorage with a distinct temp dir, so per-instance locks are both correct and sufficient. Production code with one RepositoryStorage per repo root is unaffected.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py::TestDependencyValidationAndRuntimeResilience::test_scheduler_skips_corrupt_ready_bead_with_missing_dependency` passes without worker crash; (2) `tests/test_recovery_flow.py::RecoveryBeadCreationTests::test_unrelated_failure_uses_corrective_not_recovery` passes without worker crash; (3) `tests/test_scheduler_beads.py::test_concurrent_writes_produce_no_index_lock_errors` still passes (the concurrent-write test itself must not be broken by the per-instance lock); (4) full `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes.

conflict_risks: None — only one file changed, no shared scope with other in-progress beads in this feature tree.

Moved `_git_lock` from a class-level attribute to an instance attribute in `RepositoryStorage.__init__`. Each `RepositoryStorage` instance now owns its own lock, preventing cross-test contamination when multiple test instances exist in the same pytest-xdist worker process.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.

last_agent_result.summary: Moved `_git_lock` from a class-level attribute to an instance attribute in `RepositoryStorage.__init__`. Each `RepositoryStorage` instance now owns its own lock, preventing cross-test contamination when multiple test instances exist in the same pytest-xdist worker process.
